title: fagopyrum × kuntzei
intro: Fagopyrum × kuntzei, also known as barnyard millet, is a type of hybrid plant in the family Polygonaceae, cultivated for its edible seeds and medicinal properties. The plant is known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and improving digestion. The seeds and leaves of Fagopyrum × kuntzei can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant's seeds can be ground into flour for use in baking, providing a nutritious and gluten-free alternative to traditional grains.
